description: Hand-curated QA evaluation set for the AI-policy QA app. Ground truth was authored by directly reading the NCSL corpus (data/ncsl/us_ai_legislation_ncsl_text.jsonl). 100 questions: 25 per difficulty tier (easy, medium, hard, very_hard). Bill ID whitespace is collapsed at scoring time, so padding in expected_bill_ids does not need to match retrieval output exactly.

scoring_notes.required_keywords: Fraction of listed keywords (case-insensitive substring) found in answer text. 1.0 = all found.
scoring_notes.forbidden_keywords: If any listed keyword is found in answer, penalty applied.
scoring_notes.expected_bill_ids: Set-level precision/recall of cited bill_ids vs this set (normalized by collapsing whitespace).
scoring_notes.expected_count: Single integer expected. Answer graded by ratio to expected, clipped to [0, 1]. Tolerances allow partial credit.
scoring_notes.min_citations: Hard check on number of citation chunks returned.
scoring_notes.keyword_threshold: Minimum fraction of required_keywords that must be present for the question to be considered 'passed'.

questions.question: What is the title of Delaware House Bill 16 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Bill title is 'Artificial Intelligence Commission'.

questions.question: What is the status of Kentucky Senate Bill 4 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: KY S 4 status is 'Enacted - Act No. 66'.

questions.question: In what year was Texas House Bill 149 (Regulation of the Use of Artificial Intelligence) enacted?

ground_truth.notes: TX H 149 was enacted in 2025.

questions.question: What topics does California Senate Bill 53 (Artificial Intelligence Models: Large Developers) address?

ground_truth.notes: Official topics: Cybersecurity, Government Use, Impact Assessment, Private Sector Use, Studies.

questions.question: Which California bill passed in 2025 is known as the California AI Transparency Act?

ground_truth.notes: Assembly Bill 853 (CA A 853), Enacted - Act No. 2025-674.

questions.question: Which US state enacted the 'Right to Compute Act' in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Montana Senate Bill 212, Enacted - Act No. 150.

questions.question: What is the title of Arkansas House Bill 1876 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Title: 'Model Training and Content Generated by Generative AI'.

questions.question: Was Virginia House Bill 2124 (Synthetic Digital Content) from 2025 enacted into law?

ground_truth.notes: VA H 2124 is 'Enacted - Act No. 398'.

questions.question: Was Utah's 2025 'Artificial Intelligence Consumer Protection Amendments' (bill 226) a House bill or a Senate bill?

ground_truth.notes: Utah S 226 is a Senate bill.

questions.question: In what year was Tennessee's 'Preventing Deepfake Images Act' enacted?

ground_truth.notes: TN S 1346, Enacted - Act No. 466, 2025.

questions.question: Which US state enacted the 'California AI Transparency Act' in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: California enacted AB 853 (title: California AI Transparency Act).

questions.question: Which California Senate bill from 2025 enacts the 'Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act'?

ground_truth.notes: California SB 53; summary says 'Enacts the Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act'.

questions.question: What topics does Arkansas House Bill 1876 (2025) address?

ground_truth.notes: Official topics: Private Sector Use, Provenance.

questions.question: Which US state enacted the 'Frank Broyles Publicity Rights Protection Act' in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Arkansas HB 1071, Enacted - Act No. 159.

questions.question: What is the title of North Dakota House Bill 1429 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Title: 'Harassment and Stalking with a Robot'.

questions.question: What is the status of Nevada Assembly Bill 325 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: NV A 325 status is 'Enacted - Act No. 123'.

questions.question: What is the title of Oregon House Bill 3936 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Title: 'Security of State Assets'.

questions.question: In what year was Utah's original 'Artificial Intelligence Policy Act' (SB 149) enacted?

ground_truth.notes: UT SB 149 was enacted in 2024.

questions.question: What was the final status of Florida House Bill 919 ('Artificial Intelligence Use in Political Advertising') from 2024?

ground_truth.notes: FL HB 919 was Enacted in 2024.

questions.question: What is the title of Washington Senate Bill 5838 from 2024?

ground_truth.notes: WA SB 5838 title: 'Artificial Intelligence Task Force', Enacted 2024.

questions.question: Which US state enacted the 'Artificial Intelligence Policy Act' in 2024?

ground_truth.notes: Utah Senate Bill 149 (2024) created the Artificial Intelligence Policy Act.

questions.question: What is the title of West Virginia House Bill 3187 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Title: 'Task Force on Artificial Intelligence'.

questions.question: Which US state enacted a bill titled 'Companion Chatbots' in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: California SB 243, Enacted - Act No. 2025-677.

questions.question: What is the title of Maryland House Bill 956 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Title: 'Workgroup on Artificial Intelligence Implementation'.

questions.question: In what year was Tennessee's 'Artificial Intelligence Advisory Council' (House Bill 2325) enacted?

ground_truth.notes: TN HB 2325 was enacted in 2024.

questions.question: Under Arizona Senate Bill 1295, what class of felony is criminal impersonation using computer-generated voice or video?

ground_truth.notes: Summary describes the offense as a class 5 or 6 felony.

questions.question: What body does Mississippi Senate Bill 2426 (2025) establish, and what is its charge?

ground_truth.notes: Establishes the Artificial Intelligence Regulation Task Force to study AI applications, risks, and policy.

questions.question: What change does Delaware House Bill 16 (2025) make to the Artificial Intelligence Commission's membership?

ground_truth.notes: Adds a high school student as a non-voting member of the Commission.

questions.question: Under New York Senate Bill 822, what must state agencies do regarding automated employment decision making tools?

ground_truth.notes: State agencies must publish a list of automated employment decision making tools on the agency's website; also maintain an AI inventory.

questions.question: What does Montana Senate Bill 25 (2025) regulate?

ground_truth.notes: Regulates the use of deepfakes in election and electioneering communications.

questions.question: Under California Senate Bill 243 (Companion Chatbots, 2025), what disclosure must operators make to users?

ground_truth.notes: Operators must issue a clear and conspicuous notification that the companion chatbot is artificially generated and not human.

questions.question: Whom does Utah House Bill 452 (2025) primarily protect?

ground_truth.notes: Protects users of mental health chatbots that use AI technology.

questions.question: What kind of penalties does Texas House Bill 149 (2025) authorize for violations of AI regulation?

ground_truth.notes: Summary: 'relates to the regulation of the use of artificial intelligence systems in this state, provides civil penalties.'

questions.question: Under Oregon House Bill 3936 (2025), AI software owned or developed by what kind of corporate entity is prohibited on state IT assets?

ground_truth.notes: Prohibits AI developed/owned by a corporate entity incorporated or registered under the laws of a foreign country.

questions.question: Under Arkansas House Bill 1876 (2025), who owns content generated by a generative AI tool?

ground_truth.notes: The person who provides the input or directive to the generative AI tool is the owner (with exceptions).

questions.question: Under Nevada Assembly Bill 406 (2025), what functions are public schools prohibited from using AI to perform?

ground_truth.notes: Prohibits AI performing functions/duties of school counselor, psychologist, or social worker related to mental health.

questions.question: Under Kentucky Senate Bill 4 (2025), what must a department or agency do when it uses generative AI to render decisions about citizens or businesses?

ground_truth.notes: Must disclose through a clear and conspicuous disclaimer.

questions.question: Under Virginia House Bill 1642 (2025), what role must a qualified human decision maker play with respect to AI-based criminal justice risk assessments?

ground_truth.notes: Requires that recommendations or predictions from any AI-based tool be reviewed and approved by a qualified human decision maker before use.

questions.question: What process does Maryland House Bill 820 (2025) regulate AI's use in?

ground_truth.notes: Regulates AI/algorithm use when conducting utilization review by health carriers, PBMs, and private review agents.

questions.question: Under Virginia House Bill 2724 (2025), under what circumstances may a law enforcement agency use an automatic license plate recognition system?

ground_truth.notes: Use is permitted only as part of an active criminal investigation or investigation related to missing/endangered persons or human trafficking.

questions.question: Under New York Senate Bill 822 (2025), can a state agency's use of AI systems affect employees' existing rights under a collective bargaining agreement?

ground_truth.notes: Summary: 'the use of AI systems shall not affect the existing rights of employees pursuant to an existing collective bargaining agreement'.

questions.question: Under California Assembly Bill 316 (2025), what kind of defense is a defendant prohibited from asserting?

ground_truth.notes: Prohibits a defendant who developed, modified, or used AI from asserting that the AI autonomously caused the harm.

questions.question: Under California Assembly Bill 853 (California AI Transparency Act, 2025), what must a large online platform do with respect to provenance data on content?

ground_truth.notes: Required to detect whether provenance data compliant with widely adopted specifications is embedded/attached to distributed content.

questions.question: Which national AI risk management framework does Montana Senate Bill 212 ('Right to Compute Act', 2025) reference as a guide for deployers of critical AI systems?

ground_truth.notes: References the AI Risk Management Framework from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST).

questions.question: What does Arkansas Senate Bill 612 (2025) create?

ground_truth.notes: Creates a private right of action for the use of a design, algorithm, or feature by a social media platform that causes harm; imposes a civil penalty.

questions.question: What did West Virginia House Bill 3187 (2025) add to the agenda of the state AI Task Force?

ground_truth.notes: Adds identification of economic opportunities to the Task Force's agenda.

questions.question: Under California Assembly Bill 325 (2025), what does the Cartwright Act now make unlawful with respect to algorithms?

ground_truth.notes: Makes it unlawful to use or distribute a common pricing algorithm as part of a trust/conspiracy to restrain trade.

questions.question: What act does Nebraska Legislative Bill 504 (2025) adopt?

ground_truth.notes: Adopts the Age-Appropriate Online Design Code Act.

questions.question: Under California Senate Bill 524 (2025), what must a law enforcement officer include when submitting a report generated wholly or partially using AI?

ground_truth.notes: Reports must include a disclosure statement and the signature of the law enforcement officer/member who prepared the report.

questions.question: Under Nevada Assembly Bill 325 (2025), what must emergency management plans ensure with respect to AI?

ground_truth.notes: Plans must ensure final decisions about emergency response and resource allocation are not made by AI.

questions.question: List the AI-related bills that Delaware enacted in 2025.

ground_truth.notes: Exactly two 2025 Delaware enacted bills: DE H 16 (AI Commission) and DE H 105 (Employment Practices).

questions.question: Which US states enacted AI-related bills that specifically address elections in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: 6 states, 7 bills under the Elections topic in 2025 enacted.

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills were enacted across US states and territories in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: 192 bills with status starting 'Enacted' in year 2025.

questions.question: Which US state had the most AI-related bills enacted in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: California with 24 enacted 2025 AI bills.

questions.question: Name several 2025 enacted US state bills that specifically target deepfakes or synthetic media.

ground_truth.notes: At least 12 enacted 2025 bills with 'deepfake' or 'synthetic' in title or clearly addressing synthetic media. Partial recall acceptable.

questions.question: What did California enact in 2025 concerning large AI model developers?

ground_truth.notes: CA SB 53, Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act.

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills did California enact in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: 24 enacted California 2025 AI bills.

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills did Texas enact in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: 13 enacted Texas 2025 AI bills.

questions.question: Approximately how many 2025 enacted AI-related bills are categorized under the 'Health Use' topic?

ground_truth.notes: 19 bills in 2025 enacted set carry the Health Use topic.

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills were enacted across US states in 2024?

ground_truth.notes: 64 bills with status starting 'Enacted' in year 2024.

questions.question: List the 2025 enacted Utah bills whose titles explicitly mention 'artificial intelligence'.

ground_truth.notes: UT S 180, S 226, S 271, S 332, H 452. Five Utah 2025 enactments have 'artificial intelligence' in their title.

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills did North Dakota enact in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: 12 enacted North Dakota 2025 AI bills.

questions.question: Which 2025 enacted US state bills regulate the use of AI or algorithmic tools in health insurance utilization review or prior authorization?

ground_truth.notes: MD H 820 (utilization review), IN S 480 (prior auth), AZ H 2175 (prior auth/claim denial), NE L 77 (prior auth transparency), TX S 815 (AI in health benefit plan review). Partial recall acceptable.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted state bills that regulate law enforcement's own use of AI systems.

ground_truth.notes: CA S 524, UT S 180, ND H 1613 (drones/robots), VA H 2724 (ALPR), VA H 1642 (criminal justice risk). Partial recall acceptable.

questions.question: Approximately how many 2025 enacted AI bills address elections (by topic or by title)?

ground_truth.notes: 7 bills under the Elections topic in 2025 enacted.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted state bills that establish new AI-focused task forces, commissions, or workgroups.

ground_truth.notes: MS S 2426 (AI Regulation Task Force), MD H 956 (AI Implementation Workgroup), WV H 3187 (AI Task Force), DE H 16 (amends AI Commission).

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills did Utah enact in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: 11 enacted Utah 2025 AI bills.

questions.question: List the 2025 enacted state bills that require AI chatbots to disclose their non-human nature to users.

ground_truth.notes: CA S 243 (Companion Chatbots), UT H 452 (mental health chatbots), ME H 1154 (Transparency in Consumer Transactions).

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills were enacted across US states in 2023?

ground_truth.notes: 20 bills with status starting 'Enacted' in year 2023.

questions.question: Which five US states enacted the most AI-related bills in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Top 5 by enactments: California (24), Texas (13), North Dakota (12), Utah (11), Montana (9).

questions.question: Approximately how many 2025 enacted US state AI bills have 'deepfake', 'deep fake', or 'synthetic' in their title?

ground_truth.notes: 13 enacted 2025 bills with deepfake/synthetic in title.

questions.question: Approximately how many 2025 enacted AI bills are categorized under the 'Provenance' topic?

ground_truth.notes: 7 enacted 2025 bills carry the Provenance topic.

questions.question: Which US states enacted 2025 AI-related bills that create a private right of action for affected individuals?

ground_truth.notes: Six states with 2025 enacted bills tagged under 'Private Right of Action'.

questions.question: Approximately how many 2025 enacted AI bills carry the 'Child Pornography' topic tag?

ground_truth.notes: 20 enacted 2025 bills carry the Child Pornography topic.

questions.question: Approximately how many 2024 enacted US state bills have 'artificial intelligence' in their title?

ground_truth.notes: 16 enacted 2024 bills with 'Artificial Intelligence' in title.

questions.question: What are the bills that are about AI that are passed or enacted in 2025? What do they have in common?

ground_truth.notes: Recurring themes: deepfakes/synthetic media, election integrity, disclosure requirements, task forces/commissions, consumer protection, child safety. 192 enacted bills total.

questions.question: Compare how Texas House Bill 149 and Colorado Senate Bill 4 (both 2025) approach AI regulation.

ground_truth.notes: TX HB 149: broader AI regulation with civil penalties. CO SB 4: algorithmic systems transparency, effective by June 30, 2026.

questions.question: What common approach do Rhode Island HB 5872, Montana SB 25, and North Dakota HB 1167 share?

ground_truth.notes: All three regulate AI/deepfake/synthetic media in election communications.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted bills that address the use of AI in employment, and what do they require of employers or state agencies?

ground_truth.notes: NY SB 822 requires state agencies to publish lists of automated employment decision making tools; DE HB 105 concerns salary range disclosures.

questions.question: Compare how California Senate Bill 243 (Companion Chatbots) and Utah House Bill 452 (both 2025) approach AI chatbot transparency.

ground_truth.notes: CA SB 243: companion chatbots must give clear notification they're AI, not human (broad consumer scope). UT HB 452: targeted to mental health chatbots, requires disclosures, protections, and policy requirements with Division of Consumer Protection enforcement.

questions.question: Compare how California Assembly Bill 853 (California AI Transparency Act) and Arkansas House Bill 1876 approach AI-generated content (both 2025).

ground_truth.notes: CA AB 853: large platforms must detect provenance data; GenAI system hosting platforms must place disclosures. AR HB 1876: governs ownership — person providing input/directive owns the AI-generated content.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted bills that regulate AI in health insurance utilization review or prior authorization, and what do they require?

ground_truth.notes: MD H 820 (AI/algorithm use in utilization review), IN S 480 (prior auth limits), AZ H 2175 (medical necessity denial review), NE L 77 (prior auth transparency), TX S 815 (AI in health benefit plan review).

questions.question: Compare the year-over-year growth of AI-related enactments from 2023 through 2025 in the corpus.

ground_truth.notes: Enactments: 2023=20, 2024=64, 2025=192. Roughly 3x growth each year.

questions.question: Compare Utah Senate Bill 149 (2024, 'Artificial Intelligence Amendments') and Utah Senate Bill 332 (2025, 'Artificial Intelligence Policy Act Repeal Date'). How does the 2025 bill relate to the 2024 bill?

ground_truth.notes: UT SB 149 (2024) created the Artificial Intelligence Policy Act. UT SB 332 (2025) extends that act's repeal date.

questions.question: Which 2025 enacted state bills explicitly address political or campaign advertising, and what common feature do they share?

ground_truth.notes: Common feature: mandatory disclosure of AI-generated content in political/campaign communications.

questions.question: Compare California Senate Bill 524 and Utah Senate Bill 180 (both 2025) in how they regulate law enforcement use of AI.

ground_truth.notes: CA SB 524: mandates policies for AI-generated reports with disclosure and officer signature. UT SB 180: requires a law enforcement agency policy for generative AI and specifies reporting duties.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted bills that establish or modify AI-focused governance bodies (task forces, commissions, workgroups, advisory councils). What common structure do they share?

ground_truth.notes: All create or modify multi-stakeholder bodies to study AI, recommend policy, and/or monitor implementation.

questions.question: Compare Virginia House Bill 1642 (criminal justice risk assessments) and Maryland House Bill 820 (health insurance utilization review) as examples of mandatory human oversight of AI (both 2025).

ground_truth.notes: Both require human involvement when AI/algorithmic tools drive decisions in high-stakes domains: criminal justice (VA) and health utilization review (MD).

questions.question: List several 2025 enacted state bills concerning AI-generated intimate or sexual imagery, and note what remedies they provide.

ground_truth.notes: Across these, remedies include criminal penalties (class A misdemeanor through felony) and private civil causes of action for depicted individuals.

questions.question: Compare how Colorado Senate Bill 4 (2025) and Colorado Senate Bill 205 (2024) approach AI consumer protection.

ground_truth.notes: CO SB 205 (2024) imposes duties on developers of high-risk AI to avoid algorithmic discrimination; CO SB 4 (2025) extends / modifies the algorithmic systems transparency regime (effective by June 30, 2026).

questions.question: List 2025 enacted state bills that specifically target AI use in education, and what do they share?

ground_truth.notes: IL S 1920 (state AI guidance for schools), IL H 1859 (community college AI policies), NV A 406 (school counselor/psychologist functions), WV H 2411 (CS education including AI). Shared: govern appropriate AI use in schools.

questions.question: Compare Montana Senate Bill 212 ('Right to Compute Act') and Texas House Bill 149 (both 2025) in how broadly they regulate AI.

ground_truth.notes: MT SB 212 imposes a risk-management-framework duty on deployers of critical AI (referencing NIST). TX HB 149 is a broader AI regulation framework with civil penalties.

questions.question: Which 2025 enacted state bills impose civil penalties or create explicit civil liability for AI-related harms?

ground_truth.notes: TX HB 149 (civil penalties), AR SB 612 (civil penalty + private right of action), NH HB 143 (private right of action), CO SB 288 (cause of action for non-consensual intimate digital depictions), RI SB 816 (private right of action for synthetic media).

questions.question: Compare how New York Senate Bill 822 and Kentucky Senate Bill 4 (both 2025) approach transparency of AI used by state agencies.

ground_truth.notes: NY SB 822: agencies must publish automated employment decision making tools and maintain an AI inventory. KY SB 4: agencies must disclose via clear disclaimer when generative AI is used to render decisions about citizens or businesses.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted state AI bills that specifically target children or minors, and note what remedies or restrictions they apply.

ground_truth.notes: NH H 143 (responsive generative communication to a child), NE L 504 (Age-Appropriate Design Code), ND H 1386 (computer-generated images + class C felony), TX H 581 (artificial sexual material harmful to minors), NV S 263 (pornography involving minors w/ computer-generated images), AZ H 2678 (dangerous crimes against children including visual depictions).

questions.question: Compare Delaware House Bill 16 (2025) and Tennessee House Bill 2325 (2024). How do the two states' advisory bodies differ?

ground_truth.notes: DE HB 16 amends DE's AI Commission (adds a high school student as non-voting member). TN HB 2325 creates an AI Advisory Council to recommend an action plan for AI in state government.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted state bills that concern the use of AI by emergency, law enforcement, or public safety entities, and what common oversight do they require?

ground_truth.notes: Common feature: AI cannot make final decisions without human review; agencies must disclose AI use and retain policies.

questions.question: Compare Arkansas House Bill 1071 (Frank Broyles Publicity Rights Protection Act) and Montana House Bill 513 (Name, Voice, and Likeness Property Rights) (both 2025).

ground_truth.notes: Both protect an individual's image, voice, or likeness against unauthorized commercial reproduction by AI. AR limits liability for service providers without actual knowledge; MT imposes general damages for unauthorized use without consent.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted state AI bills that create private rights of action, and identify what kinds of harms each action covers.

ground_truth.notes: AR SB 612: social media algorithmic harm. CA SB 243: companion chatbot safety. CO SB 288: non-consensual intimate digital depictions. KY SB 4: AI used by state agencies. RI SB 816: deceptive synthetic media in elections. UT SB 271: unauthorized AI impersonation.

questions.question: Across 2023, 2024, and 2025, which year showed the largest relative increase in AI enactments, and what topics drove the 2025 expansion?

ground_truth.notes: 2025 shows the largest absolute increase (64 -> 192, i.e. 3x). Drivers: deepfakes/synthetic media (13 title-level), elections (7), health use (19), education use (29), and child-safety framings.
